name: Temporal MIDI Quantizer
description: This algorithm is a MIDI quantizer, but not a pitch quantizer – it quantizes the position of notes in time (much like applying quantization to the timing of a MIDI clip in your DAW). Timing can be derived from MIDI clock or from clock pulses on the algorithm's input bus. It only affects MIDI notes – other MIDI messages are not processed. Because there is no concept of 'replacing' or 'consuming' MIDI events in the disting NT, you will usually want to use different input and output MIDI channels e.g. receive MIDI notes from your keyboard on channel 1 and output quantized notes on channel 2, and then have your synth algorithm(s) respond on channel 2.
gui_description: MIDI quantizer that adjusts note timing based on clock sources

parameters.name: Sync
parameters.description: The timing source to sync to: None, MIDI, or Clock.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

parameters.name: Input channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel on which to receive notes.

parameters.name: Output channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel on which to send notes.

parameters.name: Divisor
parameters.description: The MIDI clock divisor, if Sync is set to MIDI.

parameters.name: Prune short notes
parameters.description: If enabled, notes that are already over by the time the clock pulse comes around are suppressed.

input_ports.name: MIDI Input
input_ports.description: MIDI input for notes and clock signals

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use for clock if Sync is set to Clock.
